text: Neuroscience
the study of the nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and nerves
original_text: Neuroscience is the study of the nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.

text: asks how the brain creates thoughts, feelings, movement, memory, and behavior
original_text: Neuroscience asks how the brain creates thoughts, feelings, movement, memory, and behavior.

text: the brain is the body's main control center
original_text: The brain is the body's main control center.

text: the spinal cord carries messages between the brain and the body
original_text: The spinal cord carries messages between the brain and the body.

text: nerves connect the central nervous system to muscles, organs, and sensory receptors
original_text: Nerves connect the central nervous system to muscles, organs, and sensory receptors.

text: neurons are specialized cells that send and receive information
original_text: Neurons are specialized cells that send and receive information.

text: neurons communicate using electrical signals and chemical messengers
original_text: Neurons communicate using electrical signals and chemical messengers.

text: dendrites receive signals from other neurons
original_text: Dendrites receive signals from other neurons.

text: axons carry signals away from the neuron's cell body
original_text: Axons carry signals away from the neuron's cell body.

text: synapses are tiny gaps where neurons communicate
original_text: Synapses are tiny gaps where neurons communicate.

text: neurotransmitters are chemicals that carry signals across synapses
original_text: Neurotransmitters are chemicals that carry signals across synapses.

text: dopamine is involved in reward, motivation, movement, and learning
original_text: Dopamine is involved in reward, motivation, movement, and learning.

text: serotonin is involved in mood, sleep, appetite, and many body processes
original_text: Serotonin is involved in mood, sleep, appetite, and many body processes.

text: acetylcholine is involved in muscle movement, attention, and memory
original_text: Acetylcholine is involved in muscle movement, attention, and memory.

text: GABA is a major inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain
original_text: GABA is a major inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain.

text: glutamate is a major excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain
original_text: Glutamate is a major excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain.

text: the nervous system includes the central and peripheral nervous systems
original_text: The nervous system includes the central and peripheral nervous systems.

text: the central nervous system includes the brain and spinal cord
original_text: The central nervous system includes the brain and spinal cord.

text: the peripheral nervous system includes nerves outside the brain and spinal cord
original_text: The peripheral nervous system includes nerves outside the brain and spinal cord.

text: the autonomic nervous system controls many automatic body functions
original_text: The autonomic nervous system controls many automatic body functions.

text: the sympathetic nervous system prepares the body for action
original_text: The sympathetic nervous system prepares the body for action.

text: the parasympathetic nervous system helps the body rest, recover, and digest
original_text: The parasympathetic nervous system helps the body rest, recover, and digest.

text: the cerebral cortex is involved in thinking, perception, language, and voluntary movement
original_text: The cerebral cortex is involved in thinking, perception, language, and voluntary movement.

text: the frontal lobe supports planning, decision-making, movement, and impulse control
original_text: The frontal lobe supports planning, decision-making, movement, and impulse control.

text: the parietal lobe processes touch, body position, and spatial information
original_text: The parietal lobe processes touch, body position, and spatial information.

text: the temporal lobe is involved in hearing, language, and memory
original_text: The temporal lobe is involved in hearing, language, and memory.

text: the occipital lobe processes visual information
original_text: The occipital lobe processes visual information.

text: the cerebellum helps coordinate movement, balance, and motor learning
original_text: The cerebellum helps coordinate movement, balance, and motor learning.

text: the brainstem controls many basic survival functions
original_text: The brainstem controls many basic survival functions.

text: the thalamus helps relay sensory information to the cortex
original_text: The thalamus helps relay sensory information to the cortex.

text: the hypothalamus helps regulate hunger, thirst, temperature, hormones, and sleep
original_text: The hypothalamus helps regulate hunger, thirst, temperature, hormones, and sleep.

text: the hippocampus is important for forming new memories
original_text: The hippocampus is important for forming new memories.

text: the amygdala is involved in emotion, threat detection, and emotional memory
original_text: The amygdala is involved in emotion, threat detection, and emotional memory.

text: the basal ganglia are involved in movement, habit, and reward learning
original_text: The basal ganglia are involved in movement, habit, and reward learning.

text: brain regions work in networks rather than acting completely alone
original_text: Brain regions work in networks rather than acting completely alone.

text: neuroplasticity is the brain's ability to change with experience
original_text: Neuroplasticity is the brain's ability to change with experience.

text: learning can strengthen connections between neurons
original_text: Learning can strengthen connections between neurons.

text: repeated practice can make neural pathways more efficient
original_text: Repeated practice can make neural pathways more efficient.

text: memory depends on changes in brain connections over time
original_text: Memory depends on changes in brain connections over time.

text: attention helps the brain prioritize important information
original_text: Attention helps the brain prioritize important information.

text: the brain constantly filters sensory information
original_text: The brain constantly filters sensory information.

text: perception is the brain's interpretation of sensory signals
original_text: Perception is the brain's interpretation of sensory signals.

text: vision depends on the eyes and visual processing areas of the brain
original_text: Vision depends on the eyes and visual processing areas of the brain.

text: hearing depends on the ears and auditory processing areas of the brain
original_text: Hearing depends on the ears and auditory processing areas of the brain.

text: pain is shaped by sensory signals, emotion, attention, and context
original_text: Pain is shaped by sensory signals, emotion, attention, and context.

text: sleep supports memory, learning, immune function, and brain recovery
original_text: Sleep supports memory, learning, immune function, and brain recovery.

text: dreaming is linked to brain activity during sleep
original_text: Dreaming is linked to brain activity during sleep.

text: the brain uses a large amount of the body's energy
original_text: The brain uses a large amount of the body's energy.

text: blood flow supplies the brain with oxygen and nutrients
original_text: Blood flow supplies the brain with oxygen and nutrients.

text: the blood-brain barrier helps protect the brain from some harmful substances
original_text: The blood-brain barrier helps protect the brain from some harmful substances.

text: glial cells support, protect, and nourish neurons
original_text: Glial cells support, protect, and nourish neurons.

text: myelin helps electrical signals travel faster along axons
original_text: Myelin helps electrical signals travel faster along axons.

text: reflexes are fast automatic responses to certain stimuli
original_text: Reflexes are fast automatic responses to certain stimuli.

text: motor neurons carry signals from the nervous system to muscles
original_text: Motor neurons carry signals from the nervous system to muscles.

text: sensory neurons carry information from the body to the nervous system
original_text: Sensory neurons carry information from the body to the nervous system.

text: interneurons connect neurons within the brain and spinal cord
original_text: Interneurons connect neurons within the brain and spinal cord.

text: brain injuries can affect movement, memory, attention, emotion, and personality
original_text: Brain injuries can affect movement, memory, attention, emotion, and personality.

text: strokes happen when blood flow to part of the brain is blocked or interrupted
original_text: Strokes happen when blood flow to part of the brain is blocked or interrupted.

text: seizures involve abnormal electrical activity in the brain
original_text: Seizures involve abnormal electrical activity in the brain.

text: neurodegenerative diseases involve progressive damage to nervous system cells
original_text: Neurodegenerative diseases involve progressive damage to nervous system cells.

text: parkinson's disease affects movement and is linked to dopamine-producing neurons
original_text: Parkinson's disease affects movement and is linked to dopamine-producing neurons.

text: alzheimer's disease affects memory, thinking, and brain function over time
original_text: Alzheimer's disease affects memory, thinking, and brain function over time.

text: mental health conditions involve brain, body, environment, and experience
original_text: Mental health conditions involve brain, body, environment, and experience.

text: stress affects the nervous system and can influence attention, sleep, and emotion
original_text: Stress affects the nervous system and can influence attention, sleep, and emotion.

text: the brain and body constantly communicate with each other
original_text: The brain and body constantly communicate with each other.

text: hormones can influence brain function and behavior
original_text: Hormones can influence brain function and behavior.

text: emotion is not separate from thinking; the brain mixes them constantly
original_text: Emotion is not separate from thinking; the brain mixes them constantly.

text: decision-making depends on memory, emotion, attention, and prediction
original_text: Decision-making depends on memory, emotion, attention, and prediction.

text: language depends on coordinated networks across the brain
original_text: Language depends on coordinated networks across the brain.

text: social behavior depends on perception, emotion, memory, and interpretation
original_text: Social behavior depends on perception, emotion, memory, and interpretation.

text: uses brain imaging, experiments, recordings, and behavioral studies
original_text: Neuroscience uses brain imaging, experiments, recordings, and behavioral studies.

text: MRI can show detailed brain structure
original_text: MRI can show detailed brain structure.

text: fMRI estimates brain activity by measuring changes related to blood flow
original_text: fMRI estimates brain activity by measuring changes related to blood flow.

text: EEG records electrical activity from the brain using sensors on the scalp
original_text: EEG records electrical activity from the brain using sensors on the scalp.

text: animal studies have helped reveal basic principles of nervous system function
original_text: Animal studies have helped reveal basic principles of nervous system function.

text: neuroscience overlaps with biology, psychology, chemistry, medicine, and computer science
original_text: Neuroscience overlaps with biology, psychology, chemistry, medicine, and computer science.

text: artificial neural networks were loosely inspired by biological nervous systems
original_text: Artificial neural networks were loosely inspired by biological nervous systems.

text: the brain is not a computer, but comparisons can sometimes be useful
original_text: The brain is not a computer, but comparisons can sometimes be useful.

text: consciousness remains one of neuroscience's hardest questions
original_text: Consciousness remains one of neuroscience's hardest questions.

text: scientists still do not fully understand how brain activity becomes subjective experience
original_text: Scientists still do not fully understand how brain activity becomes subjective experience.

text: the brain makes predictions about the world and updates them using new information
original_text: The brain makes predictions about the world and updates them using new information.

text: learning often involves reducing prediction errors
original_text: Learning often involves reducing prediction errors.

text: habits form when repeated behaviors become easier and more automatic
original_text: Habits form when repeated behaviors become easier and more automatic.

text: motivation depends on goals, rewards, effort, and expected outcomes
original_text: Motivation depends on goals, rewards, effort, and expected outcomes.

text: attention is limited, so the brain must choose what to prioritize
original_text: Attention is limited, so the brain must choose what to prioritize.

text: memory is reconstructive, not a perfect recording
original_text: Memory is reconstructive, not a perfect recording.

text: the brain can change throughout life, although some kinds of change become harder with age
original_text: The brain can change throughout life, although some kinds of change become harder with age.

text: development shapes the brain through genes, experience, learning, and environment
original_text: Development shapes the brain through genes, experience, learning, and environment.

text: adolescence is a period of major brain development and behavioral change
original_text: Adolescence is a period of major brain development and behavioral change.

text: aging can affect memory, processing speed, and brain structure
original_text: Aging can affect memory, processing speed, and brain structure.

text: turns invisible thought into something scientists can study indirectly
original_text: Neuroscience turns invisible thought into something scientists can study indirectly.

text: the nervous system is both electrical and chemical
original_text: The nervous system is both electrical and chemical.

text: the brain is organized, but not simple
original_text: The brain is organized, but not simple.

text: helps explain why behavior is complicated
original_text: Neuroscience helps explain why behavior is complicated.

text: connects tiny synapses to enormous human experiences
original_text: Neuroscience connects tiny synapses to enormous human experiences.

text: neuroscience the study of the skull's suspicious thinking pudding
original_text: The meatball considers neuroscience the study of the skull's suspicious thinking pudding.

text: brain lore with electrodes, scanners, and many unanswered questions
original_text: Neuroscience is brain lore with electrodes, scanners, and many unanswered questions.

text: makes every thought feel like a tiny electrical plot twist
original_text: Neuroscience makes every thought feel like a tiny electrical plot twist.

text: reveals that the brain is both brilliant and deeply weird
original_text: Neuroscience reveals that the brain is both brilliant and deeply weird.

text: asks how nervous systems create action, perception, memory, emotion, and mind
original_text: At its core, neuroscience asks how nervous systems create action, perception, memory, emotion, and mind.
